creator: Sarah Perez
title: (No AI) Online bot traffic will exceed human traffic by 2027, Cloudflare CEO says
dc:creator: Sarah Perez
content: AI bots may outnumber humans online by 2027, says Cloudflare CEO Matthew Prince, as generative AI agents dramatically increase web traffic and infrastructure demands.
contentSnippet: AI bots may outnumber humans online by 2027, says Cloudflare CEO Matthew Prince, as generative AI agents dramatically increase web traffic and infrastructure demands.

creator: Aisha Malik
title: (No AI) Meta rolls out new AI content enforcement systems while reducing reliance on third-party vendors
dc:creator: Aisha Malik

title: (No AI) A rogue AI led to a serious security incident at Meta
author: Stevie Bonifield
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/10/STK043_VRG_Illo_N_Barclay_4_Meta.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

For almost two hours last week, Meta employees had unauthorized access to company and user data thanks to an AI agent that gave an employee inaccurate technical advice, as previously reported by [_The Information_](https://www.theinformation.com/articles/inside-meta-rogue-ai-agent-triggers-security-alert). Meta spokesperson Tracy Clayton said in a statement to _The Verge_ that "no user data was mishandled" during the incident.

A Meta engineer was using an internal AI agent, which Clayton described as "similar in nature to OpenClaw within a secure development environment," to analyze a technical question another employee posted on an internal company forum. But the agent also independently publicly replied to the question after analyzin …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/897528/meta-rogue-ai-agent-security-incident)
contentSnippet:
For almost two hours last week, Meta employees had unauthorized access to company and user data thanks to an AI agent that gave an employee inaccurate technical advice, as previously reported by The Information. Meta spokesperson Tracy Clayton said in a statement to The Verge that "no user data was mishandled" during the incident. 
A Meta engineer was using an internal AI agent, which Clayton described as "similar in nature to OpenClaw within a secure development environment," to analyze a technical question another employee posted on an internal company forum. But the agent also independently publicly replied to the question after analyzin …
Read the full story at The Verge.

Meta spokesperson Tracy Clayton said in a statement to The Verge that "no user data was mishandled" during the incident. A […]

title: (No AI) Adobe’s AI image generator can now be trained on your own art
author: Jess Weatherbed
content:
![An illustrated image representing Adobe’s Custom Firefly AI Model public beta.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/Firefly-Custom-Models-Hero-Image.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Just feed your images into the bot and it’ll try to mimic specific styles and aesthetics. | Image: Adobe

Adobe is launching customizable AI image generators that can mimic specific artistic styles and character designs. The Firefly Custom Models are available in public beta starting today, allowing creators and brands to train a model on their own assets to ensure generated images follow a consistent aesthetic for characters, illustrations, and photography.

The tool aims to streamline workflows for teams and creators that need to produce high volumes of content, providing a reusable foundation that preserves visual consistency across multiple projects, instead of having to start from scratch each time. Adobe says that custom models can help pr …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/897243/adobe-firefly-ai-custom-models-image-public-beta)
